text:
Once upon a time in the enchanting forest of Willowbrook, there stood an old oak tree known as Oakheart. Its leaves whispered secrets and its roots held memories of countless woodland creatures.

One late autumn afternoon, a tiny squirrel named Spike buzzed down from oak to oak, gathering acorns. Just as Spike was packing up his treasures, a soft glow emerged from beneath Oakheart's broad belly. There lay Lily, the enchanted moonbat. Lost within the safety of her nocturnal sanctuary, she had forgotten how to fly.

Spike flew to help her, feeling a kinship with creatures lost. He couldn't bear the thought of her night alone. Gathering acorns for light, he took her home.

Inside his nest, they lay side by side, gazing up at the twinkling stars. Lily mused about her flightlessness and dreams of adventure through the forest. Spike shared his stories from the nuts he'd gathered.

Just then, old Mr. Mouse swooped in,
